title: geniostoma rupestre var. myrtifolium
intro: Geniostoma rupestre var. myrtifolium, also known as the 'Myrtle-leaved Geniostoma', is a variety of flowering plant in the Geniostoma genus, native to the Pacific Islands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Geniostoma rupestre var. myrtifolium include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction, and its leaves can also be crushed and applied topically to the affected area.
